title: Oral appliance for use with cpap headgear
abstract: An oral appliance for use by a subject while wearing a nasal CPAP mask having an occlusal opening between a buccal portion and a lingual portion of a dental tray which allows centric occlusion. The upper and lower receptacle portions of the tray have interproximal protrusions to provide intimate contact between the appliance and a user's teeth to assist in retaining the user's jaws in place and prevent them from opening. The appliance includes buccal wall which extends into the vestibule of the user's mouth to assist in creating an intraoral seal.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. An oral appliance for use by a subject while wearing a nasal CPAP mask comprising a dental tray 100 having a lingual side 111, a buccal side 113, an anterior portion 112, a posterior portion 114, a right side 116, a left side 118, an inner surface 117, an outer surface 119, an upper side 122, and a lower side 124, wherein the dental tray comprises: a buccal wall 131 having an outer surface on the buccal side 113 of the dental tray, wherein the buccal wall 131 comprises a right buccal side 135 which extends from the anterior portion 112 of the tray to the posterior portion 114 on the right side 116 and a left buccal side 138 which extends from the anterior portion 112 of the tray to the posterior portion 114 on the left side 118; a lingual wall 133 having an outer surface on the lingual side 111 of the dental tray, wherein the lingual wall 133 comprises a right lingual side 132 which extends from the anterior portion 112 of the tray to the posterior portion 114 on the right side 116 and a left lingual side 134 which extends from the anterior portion 112 of the tray to the posterior portion 114 on the left side 118; a right side joining portion 126 having a proximal end and a distal end, wherein the proximal end is attached to the posterior portion 114 of the lingual wall 133 on the right side 116 and the distal end is attached to the posterior portion 114 of the buccal wall 131 on the right side 116; a left side joining portion 128 having a proximal end and a distal end, wherein the proximal end is attached to the posterior portion 114 of the lingual wall 133 on the left side 118 and the distal end is attached to the posterior portion 114 of the buccal wall 131 on the left side 118; an occlusal opening 125 extending between the buccal wall 131 and the lingual wall 133 from the anterior portion of the tray to the posterior portion of the tray; an upper receptacle portion 230 on the inner surface 137 of the upper side 122 of the tray, wherein the upper receptacle portion 230 comprises a plurality of maxillary dentition recesses 502 for receiving maxillary dentition of the subject, wherein interproximal protrusions 505 extend inwardly from the inner surface 137 of the upper 

receptacle portion 230, and wherein each interproximal protrusion 505 is located between adjacent maxillary dentition recesses 502 in order to engage interproximal spaces between the subjectâs teeth; and a lower receptacle portion 231 on the inner surface 137 of the lower side 124 of the tray, wherein the lower receptacle portion 231 comprises a plurality of mandibular dentition recesses 504 for receiving mandibular dentition of the subject, wherein interproximal protrusions 505 extend inwardly from the inner surface 137 of the lower receptacle portion 231, and wherein each interproximal protrusion 505 is located between adjacent mandibular dentition recesses 504 in order to engage interproximal spaces between the subjectâs teeth, wherein contact between the inner surface 137 and interproximal protrusions 505 of the dental tray 100 facilitates maintenance of the subjectâs jaws in a closed position, thereby allowing the buccal wall 131 to contact the subjectâs lips and form an intraoral seal. 2. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the occlusal opening 125 forms an open channel extending continuously between the buccal wall 131 and the lingual wall 133 from the anterior portion of the tray to the posterior portion of the tray. 3. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the upper side 122 of the buccal wall 131 extends upwardly beyond the gingival margin of a predetermined tooth by about 4-6 mm. 4. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the lower side 124 of the buccal wall 131 extends downwardly beyond the gingival margin of a predetermined tooth by about 4-6 mm. 5. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the upper side 122 of the buccal wall 131 extends upwardly for a distance which is 2/3 of the distance between the gingival margin of a predetermined tooth and the top of the subjectâs vestibule. 6. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the lower side 124 of the buccal wall 131 

extends downwardly for a distance which is 2/3 of the distance between the gingival margin of a predetermined tooth and the bottom of the subjectâs vestibule. 7. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the upper side 122 and the lower side 124 of the buccal wall 131 extend to at least the alveolar mucosa of the subject. 8. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the appliance is formed from a thermoplastic material. 9. The oral appliance of claim 8, wherein the right side joining portion 126 and the left side joining portion 128 are integrally formed with the buccal wall 131 and lingual wall 133. 10. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the right side joining portion 126 and the left side joining portion 128 are each formed from a metal wire having a proximal end and a distal end, wherein the proximal end is attached to the lingual wall 133 and the distal end is attached to the buccal wall 131. 11. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the buccal wall 131 comprises an upper frenum notch 512. 12. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the buccal wall 131 comprises a lower frenum notch 514, 13. The oral appliance of claim 1, wherein the lingual wall 133 comprises an upper frenum notch or lower frenum notch. 14. A system for treating apnea, comprising the oral appliance of claim 1 and a nasal CPAP mask. 15. Use of the oral appliance of claim 1 in the treatment of apnea. 16. A method for treating sleep apnea, comprising the steps of: wearing the oral appliance of claim 1 ; and wearing a nasal CPAP mask.
